title: Bruins trade deadline live updates: Will Don Sweeney add or subtract from Boston’s roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins General Manager Don Sweeney faces decisions as the NHL trade deadline approaches, with the team at a playoff crossroads.
Boston traded Brett Harrison and Jackson Edward to the Flyers for prospects Alexis Gendron and Massimo Rizzo.
Rasmus Ristolainen and Conor Garland are potential trade targets, but high costs and market conditions influence Boston's strategy.

title: Job interviews now include AI and personality tests. Here’s what candidates should know.
author: Elaine Varelas
bullets: 82% of companies use assessment tools in hiring, including AI and personality tests, raising complexity and legal concerns.
AI-powered interview tools analyzing traits like integrity have faced legal scrutiny in Massachusetts for potentially violating lie-detctor laws.
Candidates should inquire about an employer's interview process to better prepare for modern, multifaceted assessments.

title: She’s running her 11th Boston Marathon for her son and others affected by addiction
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jill Borthwick, 72, is running her 11th Boston Marathon for the Herren Project.
She runs in memory of her son, John O'Brien, and others affected by addiction.
Borthwick was inspired after watching Chris Herren's movie 'Unguarded' at her son's suggestion.

title: ‘If he scores 34 goals, I’ll eat my hat’: Radio host’s boast in spotlight after Morgan Geekie’s milestone goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Bruins center Morgan Geekie scored his 34th goal of the season during Thursday's 6-3 loss to the Predators.
Radio host Michael Felger previously doubted Geekie's scoring ability, promising to 'eat his hat' if Geekie reached 34 goals.
Sports Hub producer Kevin Maggiore highlighted Felger's past comments after Geekie's milestone goal, sparking social media discussion.

title: Boston police search for man accused of assaulting students
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Seitu Morel, 32, is accused of assaulting students near TD Garden in Boston on March 4.
Morel also attempted to steal a bag from a charter bus near the incident location.
Police have issued warrants for Morel for this incident and a previous one in Brighton on February 25.

title: Early bookings are strong for Cape Cod summer rentals
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Early bookings for Cape Cod summer rentals are strong, with a nearly 20% increase reported by WeNeedAVacation.com.
There are about 20,000 registered short-term rentals on Cape Cod, according to the Cape Cod Chamber of Commerce.
Rental prices are rising 3 to 6 percent, with waterfront properties seeing higher increases than those farther from the beach.

title: Ordering DoorDash in Boston? Here’s how much more you’re really paying.
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston diners pay up to 80% more for food delivery via apps like DoorDash due to service fees and markups.
Restaurants face fees of 15-30% per order on delivery apps, prompting higher menu prices on the platforms.
Pick-up and dining in are cheaper alternatives, eliminating delivery fees and reducing customer costs.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test readers' knowledge of local news in Greater Boston as of March 6, 2026.
The quiz covers trending stories to help keep the community informed about current events.
Peter Chianca, general assignment editor at Boston.com, has been with the outlet since 2019.

title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: A Boston neighborhood experienced a 149% increase in needle pickup requests from 2020 to 2025, via the 311 service.
Citywide needle pickup requests rose 71% from 2022 to 2025 but were 17% lower than in 2020.
Jamaica Plain and Back Bay saw significant increases in needle requests, with South End remaining a major hotspot.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo will run the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association to honor her mother.
Cimo reflects on memories tied to her mother's cooking as she deals with her mother's Alzheimer's diagnosis.
The Boston Marathon serves as a fundraiser for causes, including Alzheimer's research supported by Cimo's efforts.
